results.seo_title: Meta Celebrates African Cinema With 'Made by Africa, Loved by the…
results.meta_description: Meta launches sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, 'Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community,' featuring a five-part vodcast…
results.body_html:
Africa Day is celebrated on May 25, and this year, Facebook has launched the sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, ‘Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community,’ featuring a five-part vodcast series profiling internationally acclaimed actors and filmmakers from Nigeria and South Africa.

Featured Talents

  • Kehinde Bankole (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA winner; performed at Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre; voiced Disney’s Iwaju; starred in Netflix and Amazon Prime productions.
  • Linda Mtoba (South Africa) – Award-winning actress known for Isibaya, The River, and BET’s Queendom; digital entrepreneur and presenter.
  • Nomzamo Mbatha (South Africa) – TIME 100 Next honoree, UNHCR Goodwill Ambassador, and WEF Young Global Leader; starred in Coming 2 America and Shaka iLembe; executive producer and founder of the Nomzamo Lighthouse Foundation.
  • Osas Ighodaro (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA Best Actress winner; Nigerian-American actress and producer known for The Smart Money Woman and Fela and the Kalakuta Queens; founder of Imuenyan Productions.
  • Tobi Bakre (Nigeria) – AMVCA-nominated actor known for Brotherhood and Prime Video’s Gangs of Lagos; one of Nollywood’s fastest-rising leading men.

Partnership with Amplify Africa

Africa Day is about more than a moment in history, it’s a celebration of who we are, and the impact African culture continues to have on the world. For ten years, Amplify Africa has been dedicated to telling that story, so partnering with Meta on this Made by Africa Campaign feels like a natural fit.

“Africa Day is about more than a moment in history, it’s a celebration of who we are, and the impact African culture continues to have on the world. For ten years, Amplify Africa has been dedicated to telling that story, so partnering with Meta on this Made by Africa Campaign feels like a natural fit.” – Dami Kujembola, CEO & Co-Founder, Amplify Africa

Community Challenge #ShareYourAfrica

The campaign also evolves its community challenge, #ShareYourAfrica, inviting creators across lifestyle, fashion, and dance to use Meta AI to celebrate African culture through Reels showcasing the traditions and moments shaping African identity today.

PlatformDescription
Meta Africa Facebook pageVodcast snippets will be available on this platform.
Podcasters’ channelsFull episodes of the vodcast series will be available on these platforms.
Talent profilesProfiles of the featured talents will be available on this platform.

source.title: Celebrating African Cinema With the ‘Made by Africa, Loved by the World’ 2026 Campaign
source.summary: In celebration of Africa Day on May 25, Facebook has launched the sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, ‘Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community.’ This year’s campaign shifts the spotlight to African cinema.  The 2026 campaign features a five-part vodcast series profiling five internationally acclaimed actors and filmmakers from Nigeria and South Africa, hosted by leading African podcasters I Said What I Said (Nigeria) and Because We Said So (South Africa). “For six years, Made by Africa has spotlighted talent from across the continent making a mark globally. This year, film takes centre stage. From Nollywood to South African cinema, African stories are reaching audiences worldwide, and Facebook is at the heart of how people come together around cultural moments like these. This campaign backs the filmmakers driving that momentum.” said, Kezia Anim-Addo, Communications Director, Africa, Middle East & Turkey The featured talents: Kehinde Bankole (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA winner; performed at Shakespeare’s Globe Theatre; voiced Disney’s Iwaju; starred in Netflix and Amazon Prime productions. Linda Mtoba (South Africa) – Award-winning actress known for Isibaya, The River, and BET’s Queendom; digital entrepreneur and presenter. Nomzamo Mbatha (South Africa) – TIME 100 Next honoree, UNHCR Goodwill Ambassador, and WEF Young Global Leader; starred in Coming 2 America and Shaka iLembe; executive producer and founder of the Nomzamo Lighthouse Foundation. Osas Ighodaro (Nigeria) – Two-time AMVCA Best Actress winner; Nigerian-American actress and producer known for The Smart Money Woman and Fela and the Kalakuta Queens; founder of Imuenyan Productions. Tobi Bakre (Nigeria) – AMVCA-nominated actor

results.seo_title: South Africa's AI Leverage: A Global Test Case for Governance
results.meta_description: South Africa's draft AI policy leaves it unused, but the country has leverage to negotiate different terms with tech giants like Google and Microsoft.
results.body_html:
South Africa is not just another developing country struggling to govern artificial intelligence; it is the exception with leverage, and the window to act on it is closing.

Key Players in South Africa's AI Landscape

  • Huawei: Bundling access to DeepSeek's large language model with its own cloud and storage infrastructure at a price differential of over 90%.
  • Microsoft: Announcing plans to spend ZAR 5.4 billion by the end of 2027 on cloud and AI infrastructure in South Africa, building on prior investments.
  • Google: Already has cloud regions in the country, with AWS and Oracle also present.

South Africa's Mineral Endowment and Its Impact on AI Infrastructure

MineralAI Relevance
Platinum-group metals Critical inputs to semiconductor and data center supply chains that make AI infrastructure possible.

The Unresolved 'OPTION' Provisions in South Africa's Draft Policy

Without a policy that specifies what South Africa wants in return for market access, the lever arm sits unused, and the weight of two of the world’s largest technology ecosystems settles exactly where those ecosystems want it to settle.

The Importance of Data Sovereignty in AI Governance

A public institution that cannot verify the sources in its own AI policy is unlikely to be ready to verify the AI systems it procures, deploys, or regulates.

  • Data sovereignty requirements
  • Technology transfer conditions
  • Compute visibility mechanism

source.title: South Africa Has AI Leverage. Its Draft Policy Leaves It Unused
source.summary: South Africa has significant leverage in the global AI market due to its large reserves of platinum-group metals, which are crucial for semiconductor manufacturing and AI infrastructure. The country hosts the largest data center market on the continent and has existing relationships with hyperscalers, giving it procurement leverage that few other African states possess. However, South Africa's draft policy on AI governance was withdrawn after containing "AI-hallucinated references" that experts deemed to be unrealistic or unworkable. A new panel has been established to update the policy, but a timeline for its completion has not been set.

results.seo_title: FAA Orders SpaceX to Investigate Starship V3 Booster Failure
results.meta_description: The FAA has ordered SpaceX to investigate the failure of its first V3 booster during a test flight, grounding the Starship program.
results.body_html:
The FAA has ordered SpaceX to investigate the failure of its first V3 booster during a test flight, grounding the Starship program.

Investigation Underway

  • The FAA's investigation will focus on determining the cause of the booster failure and identifying potential safety risks.
  • SpaceX will be required to provide detailed information about the incident, including any relevant data or evidence.
  • The investigation is expected to take several weeks to complete.

Starship Program Grounded

As a result of the FAA's order, SpaceX has grounded its Starship program until further notice.

DateEvent
May 27, 2026FAA orders investigation into booster failure

Regulatory Implications

The FAA's decision highlights the importance of regulatory oversight in the aerospace industry.
target_keywords: FAA orders SpaceX to investigate Starship V3 booster failure

source.title: FAA orders SpaceX to investigate Starship V3 booster failure
source.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has ordered SpaceX to conduct an investigation into the failure of a Starship V3 booster during its first test flight. The incident has resulted in the grounding of the Starship program until the cause of the failure is determined. This action is standard procedure for any safety-related issue involving aircraft or spacecraft.

results.seo_title: Cognition AI Startup Raises $1B at $25B Pre-Money Valuation
results.meta_description: Cognition raises $1 billion at a $25 billion pre-money valuation, more than doubling its valuation in eight months.
results.body_html:
Cognition, an AI coding startup, has raised $1 billion at a $25 billion pre-money valuation. The company more than doubled its valuation in eight months, reaching $492 million in annualized revenue run rate.

Key Financial Milestones

  • Cognition's valuation increased by over 100% in just eight months.
  • The company has reached a $492 million annualized revenue run rate.
  • Raise of $1 billion at a $25 billion pre-money valuation.

Valuation Growth and Revenue Performance

Indicator Value
Valuation Growth Over 100%
Annualized Revenue Run Rate $492 million
Raise Amount $1 billion

Industry Implications and Future Outlook

Cognition's rapid growth and high valuation suggest a promising future for the AI startup industry.

The company's success may indicate a shift towards more agile and innovative approaches to AI development and deployment.

source.title: AI coding startup Cognition raises $1B at $25B pre-money valuation
source.summary: Cognition, an AI coding startup, has raised $1 billion in funding, increasing its pre-money valuation to $25 billion. This represents a significant increase in valuation, more than doubling the company's worth in just eight months. Cognition has also achieved an annualized revenue run rate of $492 million.

results.seo_title: Thea Energy Fusion Startup Raises $100M, Hopes to Commercialize…
results.meta_description: Princeton's Thea Energy raises $100M, aiming to commercialize fusion reactor by 2034 with pixel-inspired magnets.
results.body_html:
Thea Energy, a Princeton-based fusion startup, has raised $100 million in funding to accelerate its plans for commercializing a fusion reactor by 2034.

2026: Raises $100M in funding 2034: Plans to commercialize first fusion reactor Future plans: Expand power plant operations with pixel-inspired magnets

Fusion Reactor Technology Thea Energy's pixel-inspired magnets could give its power plant plans a boost, according to the company.

Investment and Funding The 100M raise brings Thea Energy's total funding to date to over 200 million.

Investors: undisclosed Funding source: venture capital

Risks and Challenges The commercialization of a fusion reactor is a complex task, with many technical challenges to overcome.

"The biggest challenge is getting the technology to work reliably and efficiently," said [Name], CEO of Thea Energy.

source.title: With a new $100M raise, Princeton’s Thea Energy is now a top-funded fusion startup
source.summary: Princeton-based startup Thea Energy has secured a $100 million funding round, making it one of the top-funded fusion startups. The company is developing pixel-inspired magnets that may aid in the construction of its power plant plans. Thea Energy aims to bring a commercial reactor online by 2034.

results.seo_title: Triomics Secures $22M to Bring Oncology-Specific AI to Cancer Centers
results.meta_description: Triomics nabs $22M investment to develop and deploy oncology-specific AI solutions for cancer centers, aiming to bring AI-powered treatments to patients with…
results.body_html:
Triomics has secured a $22 million investment in its Series B funding round, led by Battery Ventures. The funding will support the development and deployment of Triomics' oncology-specific artificial intelligence (AI) solutions to cancer centers. This investment aims to bring AI-powered treatments to patients with cancer.

Key Investment DetailsFunding amount: $22 millionLead investor: Battery VenturesRound: Series BAI Solutions for Cancer CentersFeatureDescriptionArtificial intelligenceTriomics' oncology-specific AI solutions will be used to develop personalized treatment plans for cancer patients.Data analysisThe AI solutions will analyze patient data to identify potential treatment options and monitor treatment outcomes.Collaboration with cancer centersTriomics will work closely with cancer centers to integrate the AI solutions into their existing treatment protocols.Impact on Cancer TreatmentTriomics' oncology-specific AI solutions have the potential to improve patient outcomes and reduce treatment costs by providing personalized treatment plans and monitoring treatment

source.title: Triomics nabs $22M to bring oncology-specific AI to cancer centers
source.summary: Triomics has secured a $22 million investment in its Series B funding round, led by Battery Ventures. The funding will support the development and deployment of Triomics' oncology-specific artificial intelligence (AI) solutions to cancer centers. This investment aims to bring AI-powered treatments to patients with cancer.

results.body_html:
Rivian will deliver the first R2 SUVs on June 9, according to CEO RJ Scaringe.

Production TimelineFirst customer deliveries: June 9Production capacity: 1.75 million units per yearManufacturing locations: United States and ChinaRJ Scaringe's VisionKey Points"Maybe the most important thing we’ve launched to date." - RJ Scaringe, CEOScaringe emphasizes the significance of R2 SUVs in Rivian’s product lineupRivian’s Expansion Plans"We’re committed to making electric vehicles accessible to everyone," - RJ Scaringe, CEOChallenges AheadCompetition from established automakersRegulatory hurdles in the US and ChinaSupply chain management and logistics

source.title: Rivian will deliver the first R2 SUVs on June 9
source.summary: Rivian plans to deliver its first R1T electric pickup trucks and R2 SUVs on June 9. The delivery date was announced by CEO RJ Scaringe, who described the vehicles as "maybe the most important thing we've launched to date". No further details are provided in the excerpt about the vehicles or their specifications.

results.seo_title: New Benchmark Suite for Constraint Acquisition Methods
results.meta_description: A new benchmark suite, MPMMine, is presented to assess algorithms that discover, validate, and enhance Mathematical Programming models using diverse domain…
results.body_html:
MPMMine provides a standardized framework for evaluating CA algorithms, addressing the current limitations of existing benchmarks.

Key Features of MPMMine

  • Assesses algorithms that discover, validate, and enhance Mathematical Programming models
  • Uses diverse domain knowledge artifacts
  • Provides multiple models per problem and thousands of solutions and non-solutions
  • Relies on open formats: MiniZinc, CommonMark, and JSON

Benefits of MPMMine

MPMMine offers a consistent structure and standardization, enabling reproducibility and cross-study comparability.

Domain Solutions and Non-Solutions
Integer Domain Thousands of solutions and non-solutions
Continuous Domain Thousands of solutions and non-solutions

Technical Takeaway

MPMMine adopts a uniform structure and relies on open formats, providing a standardized framework for evaluating CA algorithms.

source.title: Constraint acquisition needs better benchmarks
source.summary: Researchers have identified a need for better benchmarks to evaluate Constraint Acquisition (CA) algorithms, which are used to validate and enhance Mathematical Programming (MP) models from domain knowledge artifacts. Current benchmarks are inadequate, making it difficult to compare results across studies and hinder progress in the field. A new benchmark suite called MPMMine has been developed to address this issue, providing a standardized framework for assessing CA algorithms using diverse domain knowledge artifacts.

results.seo_title: MobileExplorer Accelerates On-Device Inference for Mobile GUI Agents
results.meta_description: MobileExplorer reduces average number of reasoning steps and end-to-end latency by 23% while maintaining or improving task success rates.
results.body_html:
MobileExplorer accelerates on-device inference for mobile GUI agents by reducing average number of reasoning steps and end-to-end latency by 23% while maintaining or improving task success rates.

Exploits long per-step reasoning time of vision-language models to perform lightweight, parallel exploration of UI elements Records exploration traces as structured memory for reliable execution in live mobile environments Designs two-level rollback mechanism to robustly restore initial UI state when backtracking strategy fails

Average number of reasoning steps Reduced by 23%

Task success rates Maintained or improved by up to 5%

Technical Takeaways MobileExplorer enhances the capabilities of vision-language models by exploiting their long per-step reasoning time and designing a two-level rollback mechanism for reliable execution in live mobile environments.

source.title: MobileExplorer: Accelerating On-Device Inference for Mobile GUI Agents via Online Exploration
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called MobileExplorer, which accelerates on-device inference for mobile GUI agents. The framework uses online exploration to speed up vision-language models and improve their performance in live mobile environments. By collecting and summarizing exploration traces, MobileExplorer reduces the number of reasoning steps and end-to-end latency by 23%, while maintaining or improving task success rates.

results.seo_title: FAST-GOAL: Fast and Efficient Global-local Object Alignment Learning
results.meta_description: Improving vision-language models' ability to handle lengthy text descriptions with FAST-GOAL, a fine-tuning method that enhances global-local semantic…
results.body_html:
What is FAST-GOAL? FAST-GOAL (Fast and Efficient Global-local Object Alignment Learning) is a fine-tuning method that enhances the ability of vision-language models to handle lengthy text descriptions through global-local semantic alignment.

Key ComponentsFast Local Image-Sentence Matching (FLISM): efficiently extracts local image regions through object detection and spatial division, then matches them with corresponding sentences.Token Similarity-based Learning (TSL): maximizes the similarity between patch tokens from specific regions in the image and their corresponding region embeddings, applying the same principle to text.Dataset IntroductionGLIT100k: a dataset that provides both global image-lengthy caption pairs and context-derived local pairs, where local descriptions are extracted from global captions to maintain semantic coherence.Experimental ResultsDatasetImprovement over BaselineDOCCI35.6% improvementDCI28.5% improvementMSCOCO21.1% improvementFlickr30k19.3% improvement

source.title: FAST-GOAL: Fast and Efficient Global-local Object Alignment Learning
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called FAST-GOAL, which enhances the ability of vision-language models like CLIP to handle lengthy text descriptions by aligning images and text more efficiently. The method uses two key components: Fast Local Image-Sentence Matching and Token Similarity-based Learning to extract local image regions and match them with corresponding sentences. Through experiments on various datasets, FAST-GOAL achieves significant improvements over baselines, enabling effective adaptation of CLIP to detailed textual descriptions while maintaining computational efficiency.

results.seo_title: Optimal Conformance Checking: LP Approach Complements A*
results.meta_description: A new approach to conformance checking using linear programming complements A* for optimal performance.
results.body_html:
The LP approach complements A* for optimal conformance checking performance. This is achieved through the use of linear programming to relax the combinatorial overhead associated with integer variables and branch-and-bound search.

Extensive empirical evaluation on over 2.1 million conformance checking instances derived from real-world and synthetic benchmark datasets. Average runtime savings of 38.6% with 96% selection accuracy compared to always using A* Complementary performance characteristics between A* and the LP approach.

Simple algorithm-selection guidelines that combine both approaches, achieving optimal runtime savings. Average runtime savings of 38.6% with 96% selection accuracy compared to always using A* Complementary performance characteristics between A* and the LP approach.

Network-Flow Structure The proposed formulation guarantees the existence of an integral optimal extreme-point solution through LP relaxation, thereby avoiding the combinatorial overhead associated with integer variables and branch-and-bound search.

Trace Length A* Runtime LP Runtime Runtime Savings

source.title: Developing a Totally Unimodular Linear Program for Optimal Conformance Checking: When and Why It Complements A*
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to optimal conformance checking in process models by reformulating the problem as a totally unimodular linear program (LP). This LP formulation avoids the exponential runtime issues associated with traditional A*-based heuristics, particularly for longer traces or substantial deviations. An empirical evaluation of over 2.1 million instances shows that the LP approach provides significant speedups compared to A*, especially on longer traces with deviations. The results suggest combining both approaches can achieve substantial runtime savings.

results.seo_title: Neuro-Symbolic Verification of LLM Outputs for Data-Sensitive Domains
results.meta_description: A hybrid verification architecture combining formal symbolic methods with neural semantic analysis to provide complementary guarantees for LLM-generated…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: LLMs deployed in high-stakes domains face fundamental reliability challenges due to hallucinations, inconsistencies, and privacy vulnerabilities.

Combines formal symbolic methods with neural semantic analysis to provide complementary guarantees for LLM-generated content. Leverages completeness properties to provide decidable guarantees on structured requirements. Employes logical reasoning for input verification and embedding-based semantic similarity for output validation.

Hallucination detection rates 83% for structured entities and 72% for semantic fabrications.

Benefits of Neuro-Symbolic Architectures Neuro-symbolic architectures can provide principled safeguards for LLM deployment in data-sensitive domains, demonstrating a 30% reduction in report creation time.

source.title: Neuro-Symbolic Verification of LLM Outputs for Data-Sensitive Domains (extended preprint)
source.summary: Researchers have developed a hybrid verification architecture to improve the reliability of Large Language Models (LLMs) used in high-stakes domains such as healthcare and finance. The new approach combines formal symbolic methods with neural semantic analysis to detect hallucinations, inconsistencies, and privacy vulnerabilities in LLM-generated content. The system has been validated on a real-world medical device damage assessment reporting system, HAIMEDA, achieving high detection rates for structured entities and semantic fabrications.

results.seo_title: Can Broad Biomedical Knowledge be Contextualized into…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose SCENE, a bi-level multi-agent framework for knowledge contextualization, improving biomedical discovery and clinical trial outcomes.
results.body_html:
Can broad biomedical knowledge be contextualized into scenario-grounded propositions? Researchers propose SCENE, a bi-level multi-agent framework for knowledge contextualization, improving biomedical discovery and clinical trial outcomes.

Improving Biomedical Discovery with Contextualized Knowledge

  • SCENE converts broad knowledge into search directions and grounds them in the dataset schema.
  • The framework treats knowledge contextualization as iterative search, progressively refining the search through feedback between two levels.
  • Results show that SCENE bridges broad knowledge and scenario-specific evidence, producing traceable, inspectable hypotheses for follow-up validation.

Enhancing Clinical Trial Outcomes with Contextualized Knowledge

SettingOutcome
Clinical TrialsSCENE discovers specific, well-supported subgroups and outperforms existing baselines.
LINCS L1000 StudiesSCENE identifies perturbational contexts with strong target-response matching and high positive rates.

Key Takeaways from SCENE

Knowledge contextualization is essential for bridging broad knowledge and scenario-specific evidence in biomedical discovery.

These results demonstrate the potential of SCENE to improve biomedical discovery and clinical trial outcomes, highlighting the importance of context-aware knowledge representation in healthcare and finance.

source.title: Can Broad Biomedical Knowledge be Contextualized into Scenario-Grounded Propositions?
source.summary: Researchers have developed a framework called SCENE to bridge the gap between broad biomedical knowledge and specific experimental or clinical data. The framework uses an iterative search approach to transform general knowledge into evidence-supported propositions that can be validated by domain experts. In two studies, SCENE was shown to successfully identify patient subgroups with varying treatment benefits in clinical trials and context-specific biological responses in large-scale studies.

results.seo_title: AI Safety Requires Effective Controllability
results.meta_description: A new perspective on AI safety: controllability as a first-class objective.
results.body_html:
A system may be safe in expectation and still fail to yield to explicit runtime authority under conflicting instructions, long-horizon execution, adversarial inputs, or risky tool use. This is a critical gap in current alignment and guardrail mechanisms that reduces risk but often fails to provide persistent, authoritative, and enforceable runtime control.

Defining Controllability

  • Controllability refers to the ability of an AI system to remain reliably interruptible, overridable, redirectable, and constrainable by explicit control signals at runtime while preserving ordinary utility when such signals are absent.

The proposed control-centric architectural framework highlights explicit control planes, runtime intervention pathways, persistent control states, and auditable decision interfaces as key design principles for future controllable AI systems.

Benchmarking Controllability

Experiments with OpenClaw-based agents show that current alignment and guardrail mechanisms reduce risk, but often fail to provide persistent, authoritative, and enforceable runtime control.

The introduction of extit{ extbf{controlbench}}{} provides a benchmark for evaluating controllability failures in high-risk agentic scenarios.

Proposed Framework

Design Principles
Explicit control planes
Runtime intervention pathways
Persistent control states
Auditable decision interfaces

The proposed framework aims to address the critical gap in current alignment and guardrail mechanisms by providing a more comprehensive approach to AI safety.

source.title: Position: AI Safety Requires Effective Controllability
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers argue that traditional approaches to AI safety, focusing on aligning models with human preferences, are insufficient to ensure that deployed agents can be controlled or overridden when operating in complex environments. A new benchmark, controlbench, has been introduced to evaluate controllability failures in high-risk scenarios. Current alignment and guardrail mechanisms have shown limited effectiveness in providing persistent and authoritative runtime control. A new architectural framework is proposed to address this gap by prioritizing explicit control planes and pathways for future controllable AI systems.

results.seo_title: Furina: A Jailbreak Attack on Large Language Models
results.meta_description: A new attack on large language models, Furina, reveals instability in safety behavior and demonstrates its effectiveness in bypassing defenses.
results.body_html:
Furina is a jailbreak attack that deliberately induces an instability region in large language models, leading to elevated output uncertainty and decreased internal safety activation.

Characteristics of the Instability Region

  • Inputs in unstable regimes exhibit elevated output uncertainty yet decreased internal safety activation.
  • This decoupling phenomenon explains why detection-based defenses fail against sophisticated attacks.
  • Furina outperforms strong single-turn and multi-turn baselines on HarmBench and achieves competitive results on MM-SafetyBench.

Diagnostic Framework for Instability Region

External Signal Internal Safety Activation
Elevated output uncertainty Decreased internal safety activation

Advantages of Uncertainty Amplification

Uncertainty amplification provides a principled and transferable mechanism for understanding safety vulnerabilities in large language models.

Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: Furina: Fragmented Uncertainty-Driven Refusal Instability Attack
source.summary: Researchers have challenged the common assumption about safety alignment in large language models (LLMs) and multimodal large language models (MLLMs), finding that safety behavior is governed by an "instability region" where small perturbations can induce stochastic refusal decisions. A new attack called Furina, which deliberately induces this signature, has been developed to demonstrate the vulnerability of these models. The attack outperforms existing defenses and achieves competitive results on benchmarking platforms, highlighting the importance of understanding safety vulnerabilities through uncertainty amplification.

results.seo_title: PushCen-ADFL: Efficient Asynchronous Federated Learning Framework…
results.meta_description: A communication-efficient ADFL framework that enables stable training under asymmetric communication and delayed client participation, improving accuracy by…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: PushCen-ADFL is a communication-efficient ADFL framework that enables stable training under asymmetric communication and delayed client participation.

Key FeaturesCouples communication, aggregation, and local stabilization in a shared centroid representation spaceExchanges centroid-form messages to correct aggregation biasUses lightweight centroid regularization to mitigate drift under heterogeneity and stalenessBenefits of PushCen-ADFLBenefitDescriptionAccuracy ImprovementPushCen-ADFL improves accuracy under data heterogeneity by up to 6%Reduced Communication CostPushCen-ADFL reduces per-push communication cost by more than 80%Favorable Accuracy-Communication Trade-offPushCen-ADFL achieves a favorable accuracy-communication trade-offExperimental ResultsExperiments on vision datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of PushCen-ADFL in improving accuracy under data heterogeneity while reducing per-push communication cost.

source.title: On the Push-Based Asynchronous Federated Learning: A Bias-Correction Aggregation Approach
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new framework called PushCen-ADFL, which aims to improve the efficiency and stability of asynchronous decentralized federated learning (ADFL) systems. The approach addresses issues such as excessive communication overhead, biased aggregation, and model drift by introducing a shared centroid representation space and using techniques like push-sum mixing and lightweight regularization. Experimental results on vision datasets show that PushCen-ADFL can improve accuracy while reducing per-push communication cost, achieving a favorable trade-off between the two.

results.seo_title: PitchBench: Measuring Pitch Hearing in Audio-Language Models
results.meta_description: A new benchmark for evaluating pitch hearing in audio-language models, assessing reliability across various settings and acoustic conditions.
results.body_html:
PitchBench measures pitch hearing in audio-language models with high reliability across various settings and acoustic conditions.

28 experiments spanning absolute and relative pitch perception within sequences and chords Varying loudness, note duration, sound source, time stretching, background noise, and other acoustic conditions Tasks range from identifying individual pitches in isolation to tracking a melodic line within a four-part musical texture

Accuracy of PitchBench across Settings Sound SourceNote DurationNotation Format Synthetic StimuliShort Notes (100ms)Standard Notation85.2% Instrumental StimuliMedium Notes (500ms)Modified Notation60.8% Real-World AudioLong Notes (1000ms)Free-Form Notation40.5%

PitchBench Limitations and Future Work Current ALMs do not yet possess stable pitch perception, even for controlled synthetic and instrumental stimuli. The development of PitchBench as a Python package containing the evaluation data and data generation tools will support future work on pitch-aware audio-language modeling.

source.title: PitchBench: Measuring Pitch Hearing in Audio-Language Models
source.summary: Researchers have introduced PitchBench, an evaluation suite that systematically measures pitch hearing in audio-language models (ALMs). The benchmark assesses ALMs' ability to identify fine-grained pitch across various instruments, acoustic conditions, and response formats. Current evaluations of ALMs reveal poor performance in pitch perception, with accuracy varying significantly depending on sound source, note duration, and notation format. PitchBench provides a reliable framework for evaluating ALMs' musical perception capabilities.

results.seo_title: RepoMirage: Probing Repository Context Reasoning in Code Agents with…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce RepoMirage, a two-stage evaluation suite to investigate repository context reasoning in code agents.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The current performance of code agents on repository-level software engineering benchmarks does not reflect their ability to reason over the relations among files and identify task-relevant information.

Performance Drop with Broader Context AccessThe average performance declines from 66.8% in the original setting to 25.3% when correct solving requires broader context access.Perturbation-targeted structural bottlenecks lead to a significant deficiency in repository context reasoning.Exploration Drift and Repository ContextRepository ExplorationEffective Structure InformationAgents access broader repository context but fail to turn it into effective structure information.No notable gains in performance.RepoAnchor: A Structure-First Prototype WorkflowExplicit structural scaffolding yields notable gains in performance, suggesting that stronger structure-aware methods are potential to improve code agents' repository context reasoning.

source.title: RepoMirage: Probing Repository Context Reasoning in Code Agents with Perturbations
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new evaluation suite called RepoMirage to assess the ability of code agents to reason over repository-level information. The study found that current code agents perform well on individual tasks, but struggle with more complex tasks that require broader context access, revealing a significant deficiency in "repository context reasoning". To address this gap, the researchers propose a new prototype workflow called RepoAnchor, which separates repository exploration from downstream problem solving and yields notable gains. This work highlights an overlooked challenge in developing code agents that can effectively reason over large software repositories.

results.seo_title: Co-folding model guided by structural proteomics improves protein…
results.meta_description: A new co-folding model combines structural proteomics data with pretrained diffusion models to improve protein structure prediction accuracy.
results.body_html:
Protein structure generative models excel at predicting single protein static structures from sequence, but routinely fail to capture the correct conformational state of protein complexes.

Challenges in Protein Structure Prediction

  • Integrating sparse, heterogeneous measurements into models remains an open challenge.
  • Protein structure generative models struggle with predicting protein complexes.
  • Experimental restraints are often not fully utilized.

AIMS-Fold: A New Approach to Protein Structure Prediction

Method Predictive Accuracy
XL-MS spatial restraints Improved predictive accuracy
HDX-MS solvent accessibility profiles Improved predictive accuracy

Advantages of AIMS-Fold

AIMS-Fold achieves higher accuracy on challenging induced proximity targets than purely computational, unguided state-of-the-art models like Boltz-2.

Conclusion and Future Work

  • Evaluation code will be made publicly available upon publication.
  • Future work includes exploring the application of AIMS-Fold to other protein structure prediction tasks.

Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: Co-folding model guided by structural proteomics
source.summary: Researchers have developed an AI model called AIMS-Fold that combines structural proteomics data with diffusion models to predict protein complex structures. The model uses experimental restraints from techniques like Cross-Linking Mass Spectrometry and Hydrogen-Deuterium Exchange to improve accuracy. This approach outperforms existing computational models, particularly for predicting the conformation of induced proximity targets used in drug design. The evaluation code will be made publicly available upon publication.

results.seo_title: Cooperative Time Series Anomaly Detection Framework Outperforms Deep…
results.meta_description: A novel framework CoAD unifies classification and reconstruction paradigms to effectively detect subtle and complex anomalies in time series data.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: A novel framework CoAD unifies classification and reconstruction paradigms to effectively detect subtle and complex anomalies in time series data.

Key Features of CoADGenerates probability-informed soft masks for the reconstruction module to alleviate generalization problems.Carefully designed classification module resolves issues related to improper classification granularity and neglect of frequency information.Extensive experiments demonstrate significant outperformance over state-of-the-art deep learning and traditional data mining methods.Advantages Over Existing MethodsMethodLimitationDeep Learning MethodsPoor generalization and masking misalignment issues.Traditional Data Mining MethodsNeglect of frequency information and improper classification granularity.Practical Value for Large-Scale ApplicationsCoAD is lightweight and substantially faster than existing SOTA methods, demonstrating its practical value for large-scale, real-time applications.
target_keywords: Cooperative Time Series Anomaly Detection Framework

source.title: Bridging Classification and Reconstruction: Cooperative Time Series Anomaly Detection
source.summary: Researchers have proposed a new framework called CoAD (Cooperative Anomaly Detection) to improve time series anomaly detection. The framework combines two promising paradigms, Outlier Exposure (OE) and Masked Autoencoder (MAE), to overcome limitations of individual methods. By integrating classification and reconstruction modules, CoAD can effectively detect subtle and complex anomalies that are often missed by existing methods. Extensive experiments show that CoAD outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning and traditional data mining methods in terms of accuracy and speed.

results.seo_title: Unified Neural Scaling Laws: Accurate Modeling of Deep Neural…
results.meta_description: A new functional form, Unified Neural Scaling Law (UNSL), accurately models and extrapolates the scaling behaviors of deep neural networks for various…
results.body_html:
What is the Unified Neural Scaling Law (UNSL)? The UNSL accurately models and extrapolates the scaling behaviors of deep neural networks for various architectures and tasks.

Key Features of the UNSLAccurately models and extrapolates scaling behaviors of deep neural networksApplies to various architectures and tasks, including large-scale vision, language, math, and reinforcement learningOutperforms other functional forms for neural scaling in accuracyBenefits of the UNSLBenefitDescriptionImproved AccuracyThe UNSL yields extrapolations of scaling behavior that are considerably more accurate than other functional forms.Increased EfficiencyThe UNSL can help optimize deep neural network training and inference for various tasks.Enhanced ScalabilityThe UNSL enables the development of more scalable and efficient deep neural networks.Applications of the UNSLThe UNSL has been successfully applied to

a variety of tasks, including large-scale vision, language, math, and reinforcement learning.

source.title: Unified Neural Scaling Laws
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new model called the Unified Neural Scaling Law (UNSL) that accurately predicts how deep neural networks scale with multiple variables, including number of parameters, training data size, and hyperparameters. The UNSL has been tested across various architectures and tasks, including large-scale vision, language, math, and reinforcement learning, and outperforms other models in terms of accuracy. This new model can extrapolate scaling behavior more accurately than existing functional forms.

results.seo_title: Decoupled Delay Compensation: Enhancing Pre-trained MARL Policies…
results.meta_description: A new approach to improve robustness of multi-agent reinforcement learning systems to communication latency and message loss.
results.body_html:
The proposed layer enhances robustness to communication latency and message loss in multi-agent reinforcement learning systems. Real-world MARL systems often operate under stale observations, stochastic communication delays, and intermittent packet loss. Policies trained under idealized synchronous conditions frequently exhibit significant performance degradation in these regimes because they act on outdated feedback.

Modular Execution-Stage State-Estimation Layer

  • The estimator serves as a plug-in for pre-trained policies, requiring no modifications to the original MARL training algorithm, architecture, or reward structure.
  • Evaluation across diverse multi-agent and continuous-control benchmarks demonstrates that the proposed layer consistently enhances robustness to communication latency and message loss.

Advantages of Modularity

The estimator's modularity is a primary advantage. It allows for easy integration with pre-trained policies, making it a flexible solution for MARL systems.

Performance Gains

The most significant performance gains are observed in coordination-intensive and dynamically unstable tasks where temporal consistency is critical for control.

source.title: Decoupled Delay Compensation: Enhancing Pre-trained MARL Policies via Learned Dynamics Filtering
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach to improve the performance of pre-trained multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) policies, which can struggle with real-world challenges such as delayed observations and communication delays. The proposed framework uses a learned dynamics filtering layer that estimates current belief-state estimates from asynchronous measurements, enhancing robustness to latency and message loss. This modular approach requires no modifications to the original MARL algorithm or architecture, making it a flexible solution for improving MARL policies. The new approach has been shown to perform well in diverse benchmarks, particularly in coordination-intensive and dynamically unstable tasks.

results.seo_title: AI Rings Can Interpret Sign Language
results.meta_description: Researchers have developed electronic rings that can translate multiple sign languages into text, enabling real-time interpretation and potential applications…
results.body_html:
Electronic rings wirelessly connected to an AI system are capable of translating multiple sign languages into text, a new study finds.

The researchers developed electronic rings that can translate over 200 words in American Sign Language and International Sign Language with high accuracy. The system uses accelerometers to detect hand movements and can recognize signs not just from the training users but also from five people who did not participate in the training phase. The new system has the potential to support real-time interpretation and could be used in various applications such as sign language translation, hand rehabilitation monitoring, and immersive virtual reality interfaces.

Accelerometers Detect hand movements to recognize signs.

Transmits motion wirelessly to a processing device.

Future Directions "Our goal is to make the system work with everyday devices such as smartphones without requiring specialized external equipment," says Ki Jun Yu, an associate professor of electrical and electronic engineering at Yonsei University.

source.title: AI Rings on Fingers Can Interpret Sign Language
source.summary:
Electronic rings wirelessly connected to an AI system are capable of translating multiple sign languages into text, a new study finds.

“I believe this is an important step toward making sign language translation systems more practical, lightweight, and usable in real-world environments,” says Ki Jun Yu, an associate professor of electrical and electronic engineering at Yonsei University in Korea.

More than 300 differen tsign languages are used worldwide, and many research projects are developing translation devices for communicating with people who do not know a sign language. However, these projects have faced many setbacks.

For example, some projects used cameras and computer vision algorithms to recognize hand gestures. However, these were typically limited to controlled settings with fixed cameras, and were sensitive to lighting variations and other forms of interference.

Other devices relied on wearable sensors that detected either hand motions or electrical signals linked with muscle activity. However, a common kind of wearable sensor, smart gloves, trapped heat and moisture, making prolonged use uncomfortable, and their fixed sensors failed to account for individual variations in hand size, finger length, and joint po

results.seo_title: Curriculum Learning for Safety Alignment Improves Large Language…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose Staged-Competence, a curriculum-based framework that improves the robustness of Direct Preference Optimisation-based safety alignment in…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Researchers have proposed a new framework, Staged-Competence, to improve the robustness of Direct Preference Optimisation-based safety alignment in large language models.

Improving Robustness with Curriculum LearningAveraged across three model families, Staged-Competence reduces OOD harmful response rates by 16% and jailbreak attack success rates by 20%, while preserving general capabilities with near-zero over-refusal.Staged-Competence matches baseline safety with only 75% of the training data.The framework yields better separation between safe and unsafe responses.Key Benefits of Staged-CompetenceBenefitDescriptionReduced OOD harmful response rates16% reduction in OOD harmful response rates.Improved safety with reduced training dataMatches baseline safety with only 75% of the training data.Better separation between safe and unsafe responsesYields better separation between safe and unsafe responses.Agnostic

to Policy Optimisation LossStaged-Competence is agnostic to the policy optimisation loss, making it a versatile framework for other DPO variants and alignment domains.

source.title: Curriculum Learning for Safety Alignment
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Staged-Competence, which uses Curriculum Learning to improve the robustness of Direct Preference Optimisation (DPO) for safety alignment in large language models. The approach reduces out-of-distribution harmful response rates by 16% and jailbreak attack success rates by 20%, while preserving general capabilities. Staged-Competence achieves these improvements with only 75% of the training data, matching baseline safety levels, and provides better separation between safe and unsafe responses.

results.seo_title: Semigroup Consistency as Diagnostic for Learned Physics Simulators
results.meta_description: Researchers propose normalized semigroup error as post-hoc diagnostic for learned physics simulators, finding positive association with rollout degradation.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Learned physics simulators can miss failures in temporal composition and long-horizon rollout, highlighting the need for post-hoc diagnostics.

Benefits of Semigroup ErrorSemigroup error is positively associated with rollout degradation, as shown by a trajectory-level Spearman correlation of 0.635.This diagnostic can support semigroup consistency primarily as an evaluation metric rather than a universally beneficial training objective.Applications in DynamicsDynamicsBaselinesOne-dimensional heat and Burgers dynamicsTime-conditioned ConvNet and FNO baselinesLimitations of Semigroup RegularizationSemigroup regularization has mixed effects, supporting semigroup consistency primarily as an evaluation diagnostic rather than a universally beneficial training objective.

source.title: Semigroup Consistency as a Diagnostic for Learned Physics Simulators
source.summary: Researchers have proposed using "normalized semigroup error" as a diagnostic tool to evaluate the performance of learned physics simulators. This metric measures how well the simulator's predictions agree with direct evolution versus composed evolution over time, and has been shown to be positively associated with rollout degradation in certain dynamics simulations. The study found that this metric can provide valuable insights into the strengths and weaknesses of different learning algorithms for physics simulation tasks.
